other related PPE records/reports the
actual condition of the PPE as validated
during the inventory for proper
monitoring and reporting of
unserviceable properties subject to
disposal;
3) Revisit the project proposal for these E-trikes to reassess their intended purpose
and find ways to utilize them effectively; and
4) Secure the properties, particularly motor
vehicles, by placing them in the motor
pool. If the motor pool is unavailable,
explore alternative storage options such
as unused areas within the premises to
store properties when not in use, and
implementing protective measures, such
as covering the vehicles or installing
security devices to prevent damage or
theft.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the:

We recommended that Management establish which among the long outstanding cash advances totaling ₱2,600,940.74 are “Dormant Unliquidated Cash Advances” or those non-moving for at least ten years with uncertainty of collectability despite repeated demands.
We likewise recommended that Management consider subjecting these dormant Advances to request for write-off, subject to documentary and procedural requirements under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and supplemental guidelines under COA Circular No. 2022-001 in the event of subsequent settlement of accounts previously written off.

We recommended that Management:
strictly adhere with the specific guidelines/rules and regulation set forth for changes in the annual budget of LDRRMF as well as to existing budgeting rules and regulations;
submit approved ordinance/supplemental budget covering the changes in budget of LDRRMF totaling ₱5,100,000.00 to COA for review/validation; and
direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to institute necessary adjusting entries in their respective records and reports to correct the unutilized balance of LDRRMF for CY 2021 to ₱3,802,936.59.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the specific guidelines/rules and regulation set forth for the received fund transfers as well as to existing budgeting rules and regulations by appropriating thru an ordinance the additional funds received for utilization and implementation.
We also recommended the immediate submission of the aforementioned lacking supporting documents to the transactions under the LGSF-DRRAP and the liquidation of the unsettled account under NDRRM Fund amounting to ₱180,500.00 as well as the return of the unutilized funds of the cited fund transfers to their source agencies.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to return the unused fund transfers to source national government agencies and/or to the Bureau of Treasury and submit copies of journal entries and supporting documents taking up the refund to COA for validation.
We also recommended that the unreconciled outstanding balance of Due to NGAs account amounting to ₱3,385,280.10 be settled with the corresponding source agencies and submit result of action taken to COA for validation.

We recommended that Management:
strictly adhere/oblige to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20% MDF is promptly implemented and completed on target time to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU’s constituents/intended beneficiaries;
create a well-planned 20 per cent LDF PPAs which includes schedule of implementation for each projects, mode of procurement and other relevant procurement steps that usually affect the timeliness of project completion so that delays in the implementation of LDF PPAs are avoided; and
submit immediately the aforementioned lacking documents to facilitate proper contract review and technical evaluation of the undertaken MDF projects.

We recommended that Management:

conduct physical inventory of all the supplies and materials inventories and submit inventory report (RPCI) to COA; 


direct the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant to maintain the required records such as stock cards and supply ledger cards, respectively and ensure that inventory balances in the stock cards are reconciled with the ledger cards that shall support the inventory balances reported in the financial statements; and

direct the concerned management personnel in charge of the distribution of relief goods and other supplies and materials for distribution to submit the necessary reports/records, RSMI and supporting documents such as distribution lists to accounting unit for proper accounting and recording in the books.

create a Disposal Committee who shall conduct the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU;
require the accountable officers to pay the replacement cost of missing PPEs; and

instruct the accounting office to draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to adjust the erroneous recording of property, plant and equipment accounts

direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately cause reconciliation of records and immediately effect the necessary adjustments in order to properly reflect the correct account balances of the affected Due to PhilHealth and Due to NGAs accounts; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) and Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to hasten revision of the 10-year SWMP and submission of the same for corresponding approval.

We also recommended that Management require a timetable or schedule of compliance specifying the remedial measures and eventual compliance be included in the plan as prescribed in Paragraph 2, Section 16 of RA 9003.

and location site are lacking/pending

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management particularly plan and execute programs, projects and activities in accord with the priority PPAs enumerated in Item 3.0 DILG No. 2017-1.


We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and secure approval/authorization from Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract to implement projects described in generic terms.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented and/or reprogrammed projects in CY 2020

Projects specified

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to set up tax receivables based from the list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer.


We also recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) intensify collection of outstanding RPT and SET Receivables through close coordination with barangay officials; and (b) reconcile the remarked negative tax collectibles from Barangays Abo and San Francisco.
observations.managementAction:
Setup based on MTO’s list, although the latter was not based on Assessor’s RPTAR

Management was able to collect revenue amidst the pandemic.
observations.reason:
Collection efficiency was at 11.26 per cent.

No mention of the reconciliation on negative tax collectibles from the two barangays.

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

We likewise recommend that Management refrain from paying excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it that claims are supported with adequate documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan enact an ordinance to update the Local Revenue Code and every five years thereafter in accordance with RA 7160, as basis for the collection of taxes, fees and charges.
observations.reason: The draft code to be deliberated by the Sanggunian Bayan this year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure that projects to be funded by the 20% EDF are socio-economic development projects and environmental management programs categorized under Sec. 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the GSO Designate to facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials to recoup any salvage value thereof.  We likewise recommend that Management constitute the Disposal Committee who shall:

compute the appraised value of the unserviceable properties and the waste materials; and



 dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance to the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in Section 79 of RA 7160 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this Report
observations.reason: No disposition of the unserviceable PPE as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

require the prompt revision and finalization of the Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirements of the NSWMC; and


adhere strictly with the implementation of projects/programs as budgeted under the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this Report
observations.reason: Management were required to revised the submitted SWMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019 and/or implement the withholding of salary until such time the cash advances are settled in accordance with existing regulations;
stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that Management direct:
adherence to the accounting standards and method of recording supplies and materials in accordance with the above-cited provisions;
the implementation of  requisition procedures and the reporting on issuance of supplies/materials as required under Sections 114 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
the Municipal Accountant  to record the appropriate  accounts upon submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and the appropriate supporting documents.

We recommended that Management:
(a) create a Disposal Committee who shall conduct the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU;
(b)   require the accountable officers to pay the replacement cost of missing PPEs; and
(c)    instruct the accounting office to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust the erroneous recording of property, plant and equipment accounts

We recommended that Management make representations with the concerned NGAs and request for authority to utilize the unused funds to finance other priority projects otherwise, return them to the source agencies as mandated.

We recommended that Management: (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately cause reconciliation of records and immediately effect the necessary adjustments in order to properly reflect the correct account balances of the affected Due to PhilHealth and Due to NGAs accounts; and (b) require the preparation and submission to PhilHealth the required liquidation report on the utilization of PCF in accordance with PhilHealth Circular No. 40 s. 2000.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) and Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to hasten revision of the 10-year SWMP and submission of the same for corresponding approval.
We also recommended that Management require a timetable or schedule of compliance specifying the remedial measures and eventual compliance be included in the plan as prescribed in Paragraph 2, Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that Management direct the designated MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF (Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002), as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

observations.managementAction:
A certification issued by the Accountant is (no unliquidated Cash Advance) is a prerequisite for every new CA.

The case of former MT Glenn Sendon was forwarded to DOF for appropriate legal action.
observations.reason: 49.91% of the ₱8.045 million were liquidated during the year

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

strictly observe proper documentation of financial assistance claims in     accordance Section 4(6) of PD 1445; and 

establish specific policy, guidelines or criteria in the granting of all kinds of financial assistance to its constituents to ensure efficient implementation of the program and economical utilization of its limited resources.

We recommended that Management:

conduct regular monitoring of on-going construction to ensure that appraisal and assessment on the building are issued upon completion of construction; 

issue Notices of Tax Delinquency to those real properties with no payment from 2011 and earlier years and post the notices in accordance with Section 254 of 1991 LGC, to optimize collection of RPT; and 

conduct regular inspection of real properties to validate actual or predominant use of property to provide assurance that collections are based on correct, accurate and credible assessments.
observations.managementAction:
Regular inspection of new constructed building.

Issued tax delinquency thru the Provincial Assessor

Regular inspection of real properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
strictly observe proper documentation of financial assistance claims in     accordance Section 4(6) of PD 1445; and
establish specific policy, guidelines or criteria in the granting of all kinds of financial assistance to its constituents to ensure efficient implementation of the program and economical utilization of its limited resources.

We recommended that Management:
conduct regular monitoring of on-going construction to ensure that appraisal and assessment on  the building  are issued upon completion of construction;
issue Notices of Tax Delinquency to those real properties with  no payment from 2011 and earlier years and post the notices in accordance with Section 254 of 1991 LGC, to optimize collection of RPT; and
conduct regular inspection of real properties to validate actual or predominant use of property to provide assurance that collections are based on correct, accurate and credible assessments.

We recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan enact an ordinance to update the Local Revenue Code and every five years thereafter in accordance with RA 7160, as basis for the collection of taxes, fees and charges.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure that projects to be funded by the 20% EDF are socio-economic development projects and environmental management programs categorized under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books PPE worth P18.327 million and direct the GSO to include the same in the inventory report.

We also recommended that Management (a) create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee who shall conduct the physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and prepare corresponding report thereon; (b) direct the reconciliation of report with accounting records; (c) require the disposal of unserviceable properties; (d) oblige the  designated GSO to prepare and maintain property cards and renew the PARE every three years; and  (e) instruct the Municipal Accountant to record semi-expendable supplies and materials to their appropriate inventory accounts.

officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid
accumulation of the same;

waste materials; and

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate immediately an employee who is a holder of a permanent plantilla position to handle the cashiering and disbursing functions, or appoint the present cashier to a permanent position. We also recommended that Management properly secure the office of the accountable officer and provide a safe/vault for the cash on hand as an added measure against loss due theft and/or robbery.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015
observations.reason: A newly hired acting cashier for PHPTC which is under direct supervision of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer. The acting cashier does not have cash on hand or kept in the PHPTC office. All collections for the day are directly deposited to the bank. Collections after banking hours are remitted and kept in the vault located at the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the same is deposited in the next banking hours.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management constitute committee on appraisal and disposal of properties to facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials to recoup any salvage value thereof.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: the process of completing the Report :::?:ZHSSA Checklist For Disposal of Unserviceable Property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging to LDRRMF those expenditures that do not concern disaster risk management activities and/or part of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish immediately the Office of the LDRRM and appoint the mandatory personnel recommended under the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 to track the full implementation of the programs/projects and activities under the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan.

We recommended that Management: 1. Submit the masterlist of beneficiaries based on the approved funding allocations; 2. Stipulate conditions pertaining to program implementation as to a) approved amount for the SFP, b) actual number of recipients, c) date started of the SFP, d) target date of program completion or the year, and e) requirements for the identification of beneficiaries; 3. Form part of the official annexes to MOA, the masterlist and target beneficiaries; 4. Monitor and evaluate reports such as a) nutritional status of children before the start of the program implementation, b) progress on the nutritional status of children to be evaluated every three (3) months, and c) quarterly monitoring of the nutritional status; and 5. Undertake the activities and disburse the fund without delay, to conform with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 and the Omnibus guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Human Resource Office to prepare and submit to the Audit Team copies of Job Orders, Contract of Service or any other contracts and its supporting documents entered into by the Municipal Government of Tigaon within five (5) days after its execution in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive appoint immediately the lacking members of the LDRRMC in accordance with Section 11 of RA 10121.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit Annual GAD Plan and Budget on time and provide the oversight agencies the prescribed Accomplishment Report on GAD and supporting documents to comply with the requirements of the law.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to exert extra effort in collecting unpaid rentals from stallholders. We also recommended that the Market Committee recommend collecting stall rentals to be assigned in the market site for convenience of the business owners; enact ordinance updating the rate of the market stall rental to generate funds for the upkeep and maintenance of the stalls and closing of stalls for violations like non payment of rentals for three (3) months as mandated by the ordinance. The vacants stalls were to be posted and offered to the public for occupancy.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Collectibles already decreased. A designated staff for every building owned for collecting unpaid rentals was assigned in the market site for convenience of the business owners. The task force barat is diligently enforcing the collection. The vacants stalls were already posted and offered to the public for occupancy.
